title: Urostachys capillaris
taxonomy: Urostachys capillaris is classified as a species of plant in the family Lomariopsidaceae, which is part of the order Polypodiales. This classification makes Urostachys capillaris a type of fern, known for its unique characteristics and growth patterns. The family Lomariopsidaceae is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Urostachys capillaris, like other members of the Lomariopsidaceae family, has a distinctive appearance with divided leaves and a robust stem. The specific classification of Urostachys capillaris provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Urostachys capillaris is a species of plant that has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Brazil and other parts of South America.
